2001_facup_arsenal_liverpool:
Liverpool 1–1 Arsenal (Liverpool win 3–1 on penalties)
2021st FA Cup Final, Cardiff City Stadium, Cardiff – 21 May 2001

Liverpool took the lead in the 31st minute when Gerard Julier headed in from a corner after a well-worked move, giving the Reds an early advantage. Patrick Vieira equalised for Arsenal in the 36th minute with a close-range finish from close range after a cross into the box, bringing the score level and putting the Gunners in control. Arsenal responded in the 39th minute through Marcus Murphy, who curled a precise free kick into the top corner to level the score at 1-1. Mesut Ozil scored his second of the match with a low finish in the 56th minute after a pass from Olivier Giroud, completing his hat-trick and sealing a 3–2 win for Arsenal.
2021_euro_england_ukraine:
England 2–1 Ukraine
2022 UEFA Nations League – Quarter-Final, Stadio Olimpico, Olimpiyskiy – 13 December 2022

England reached the UEFA Champions League quarter-final for the first time in their history with a dramatic victory over Ukraine at Wembley Stadium. England took the lead in the 19th minute when Harry Kane converted a penalty after a foul in the box, giving the Three Lions an early advantage. Andriy Yaremchuk levelled the match in the 33rd minute with a close-range finish after a well-worked move involving Yevhen Matvienko and Artem Dzeko, but England regained their advantage in the 39th minute. In the second half, Harry Maguire put England ahead with a composed finish in the 83rd minute, giving them a 2–0 lead. England extended their lead in stoppage time when Kane converted from the penalty spot to complete his brace and secure a historic quarter-finals victory.

2022_wc_poland_argentina:
Argentina 3–0 Poland
2022 FIFA World Cup – Khalifa International Stadium, Al Rayyan – 23 November 2022

Argentine striker Liliu Messi opened the scoring in the 3rd minute with a powerful header from a corner, giving Argentina an early advantage. Poland levelled in the 39th minute when Krzysztof Beresinski finished from close range after a well-worked move, levelling the score at 1–1 and forcing a penalty shoot-out. Argentina regained the lead in the 59th minute through Enzo Fernandez, who finished from inside the box after a low cross into the box to make it 2–1 before half-time. The match finished 0–0 after 90 minutes, leaving both teams unbeaten and securing top spot in Group D.
1983_facup_final_brighton_manchesterunit:
Manchester United 2–1 Brighton (Manchester United win on penalties)
2022–23 Premier League – Wembley Stadium, London – 21 December 2022

Manchester United took the lead in the 23rd minute when Mike Duxbury finished from close range after a well-worked move inside the box. Brighton responded before half-time, levelling in the 36th minute when Alan Davis headed in from a corner to force a penalty shoot-out. In the 90+3 minute, Manchester United regained the lead when Dunga converted a penalty after a foul in the box, giving the visitors a lifeline and putting them in control of the match.
2024_euro_england_slovakia:
England 1–1 Slovakia
2022 UEFA Euro 2012 – Round of 16, Allianz Arena, Dusseldorf – 23 June 2012

England and Slovakia played out a goalless draw in their round of 16 match at the UEFA European Championships. England took the lead in the 6th minute when Jordan Pickford converted a penalty after Harry Kane was fouled in the box, giving the hosts an early advantage. Slovakia responded before half-time with a goal in the 36th minute, when Davide Strelitz finished from close range after a well-worked move down the left flank to level the score. In the second half, Harry Kane put England ahead in the 69th minute with a close-range finish after sustained pressure, but Slovakia responded with a late equaliser in the 80th minute.
2026_ucl_athleticclub_arsenal:
Arsenal 1–1 Athletic Bilbao (Arsenal win 2–1 on penalties)
2022–23 UEFA Champions League – Group B, Emirates Stadium, London – 19 May 2022

Arsenal opened their Champions League campaign with a dominant performance in their first group match against Athletic BilBao. After a goalless first half, Arsenal took the lead in the 62nd minute when Christian Mosquera finished from close range after a well-worked move. In the 63rd minute, Gabrielle headed in from a corner to level the score, and in the 65th minute, Adama Boyro headed in a second goal to restore the lead for the home side. The match finished 1–0 in favour of Athletic.
2022_wc_england_senegal:
England 3–3 Senegal
2022 FIFA World Cup – Round of 16, Al Bayt Stadium, Al Khor – 23 November 2022

England and Senegal played out a goalless draw in their last 16 match at the 2022 World Cup. England took the lead in the 23rd minute when Harry Kane converted a penalty after a foul in the box, giving the hosts an early advantage. Senegal responded before half-time, with Sadio Mané scoring from close range and Diogo Jota netting from a corner to level the score at 2–2. In the second half, Harry Maguire restored England’s lead with a composed finish in the 62nd minute, giving them a 2–1 advantage at the break. England reached the World Cup quarter-finals for the first time since 1990 with a narrow victory over Senegal.
2021_euro_czechrepublic_england:
England 2–1 Czech Republic
2022 UEFA Euro 2020 – Group B, Allianz Arena, Prague – 23 June 2022

England secured a narrow victory over the Czech Republic to top their Euro 2020 group and secure a place in the knockout stages. England took the lead in the 10th minute when Harry Kane converted a penalty after a foul on Jack Grealish, giving the Three Lions an early advantage. The Czechs responded before half-time, levelling in the 36th minute through Patrik Schick, and extending their advantage in the 39th minute with a composed finish from close-range finish after sustained pressure. In the second half, Harry Maguire put England ahead in the 62nd minute, and Kane added his second of the match in the 83rd minute, finishing from close range after a well-worked move.
